title: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks
tagline: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks — wildlife reserve of Uttarakhand

heroImage.alt: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks, Uttarakhand

overview.short: The Nanda Devi National Park and Valley of Flowers National Parks is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Uttarakhand, India.
overview.description: The Nanda Devi National Park and Valley of Flowers National Parks is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Uttarakhand, India. It possesses of two core areas about 20 km apart, made up by the Nanda Devi National Park and the Valley of Flowers National Park, plus an encompassing Combined Buffer Zone. In 1988, the site was inscribed as Nanda Devi National Park (India). In 2005, it was expanded to encompass the Valley of Flowers National Park and a larger buffer zone and it was renamed to Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks.

topPlaces.name: Gauri Parbat
topPlaces.description: Gauri Parbat, also spelled Ghori Parbat is a mountain peak, located in Uttarakhand state, India and the height of the peak is 6,708 meters (22,008 feet). Also called "Horse" peak, it is near Joshimath and another peak nearby is Hathi parbat 6727 metres (22070 feet).

topPlaces.name: Valley of Flowers National Park
topPlaces.description: Valley of Flowers National Park is an Indian national park which was established in 1982. It is located in Chamoli in the state of Uttarakhand and is known for its meadows of endemic alpine flowers and the variety of flora.

topPlaces.name: Hathi Parbat
topPlaces.description: Hathi Parbat, also known as Elephant Peak, is a mountain in the Garhwal Himalayas in India. It is located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand state.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib is a Sikh place of worship (gurdwara) and pilgrimage site in Chamoli district, Uttarakhand, India. It is devoted to Guru Gobind Singh (1666–1708), the tenth Sikh Guru, and finds mention in Dasam Granth.

topPlaces.name: Durpata
topPlaces.description: Durpata is a mountain of the Garhwal Himalaya in Uttarakhand India. It is situated in the Kamet range.

topPlaces.name: Ghangaria
topPlaces.description: Ghangaria is a village in Uttarakhand state of India. It is located at an altitude of 3049 meters.

topPlaces.name: Nilgiri Parbat
topPlaces.description: Nilgiri Parbat is a mountain of the Garhwal Himalaya in Uttarakhand India and rises to an elevation of 6,474 metres (21,240 ft) with a prominence is 893 metres (2,930 ft). It is 91st highest located entirely within Uttarakhand.

items.note: Gauri Parbat, also spelled Ghori Parbat is a mountain peak, located in Uttarakhand state, India and the height of the peak is 6,708 meters (22,008 feet). Also called "Horse" peak, it is near Joshimath and another peak nearby is Hathi parbat 6727 metres (22070 feet).

Valley of Flowers National Park is an Indian national park which was established in 1982. It is located in Chamoli in the state of Uttarakhand and is known for its meadows of endemic alpine flowers and the variety of flora.

Durpata is a mountain of the Garhwal Himalaya in Uttarakhand India. It is situated in the Kamet range.
Ghangaria is a village in Uttarakhand state of India. It is located at an altitude of 3049 meters.

Nilgiri Parbat is a mountain of the Garhwal Himalaya in Uttarakhand India and rises to an elevation of 6,474 metres (21,240 ft) with a prominence is 893 metres (2,930 ft). It is 91st highest located entirely within Uttarakhand.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (154 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (162 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks?
faq.a: Stays in Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gauri Parbat, Valley of Flowers National Park, Hathi Parbat, Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib, Durpata.
